text: EU MiCA Licenses Reach Around 230, Raising Concerns Over Market Diversity The EU has issued around 230 MiCA licenses so far as the new regulatory regime reshapes Europe's crypto industry. Germany leads with 56 licenses, followed by the Netherlands with 26 and France with 21. In France, about 40% of registered crypto service providers have not applied for a MiCA license, while some firms have withdrawn applications, sought partners or moved toward shutting down. Industry participants say MiCA has strengthened market resilience, but also reduced market diversity, with smaller crypto firms facing the greatest pressure. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Highlight Clip Strategy CEO: AI Accelerated Stretch's Development from Three Years to Eight Months Strategy CEO: AI Accelerated Stretch's Development from Three Years to Eight Months On June 19, 2026, Strategy CEO Phong Le stated in an interview with Coinage that AI played a crucial supporting role in the creation of Stretch. He believes that while traditional consultations with banks and lawyers regarding innovative products would typically result in constant rejections, the team utilized AI to directly retrieve relevant case law and financial KPIs, effectively validating the product's feasibility. This approach drastically accelerated Stretch's development, shortening the estimated three-year cycle to just eight months. However, according to official data, the price of Stretch has currently fallen to $80.84, below its $100 par value, sparking renewed attention to the effectiveness of its financing mechanism and long-term sustainability. — link

text: Strategy mNAV Falls Below 1, Undercutting Its Bitcoin Accretion Threshold As of the June 26 U.S. market close, Strategy's variable-rate perpetual preferred stock STRC closed at $74.57, down 1.48% and trading at an approximately 25% discount to its $100 liquidation preference, while MSTR closed at $82.31, down 3.54%. Meanwhile, Strategy's website showed its mNAV had fallen to 0.99, implying that the company's enterprise value had dropped below the market value of its bitcoin holdings. The figure is also well below the roughly 1.22x threshold previously identified by management as the minimum mNAV at which issuing MSTR to buy additional bitcoin remains accretive on a per-share basis. Management has previously indicated that below this level, actions such as selling some bitcoin, rebuilding cash reserves, or repurchasing MSTR could be more accretive than issuing additional common stock to acquire more BTC. — link

Aave executive Luigi D'Onorio DeMeo said Aave V4 will bring tokenized stocks to onchain securities lending, allowing users to earn borrowing fees directly without intermediaries or rehypothecation. The regulator said it is working with affected firms, including Binance, to ensure an orderly exit and protect customer assets. — link

text: Breaking: Hyperliquid Added to Singapore MAS Investor Alert List Hyperliquid said its inclusion on the Monetary Authority of Singapore's (MAS) Investor Alert List (IAL) does not constitute a ban, enforcement action, or finding of wrongdoing. The protocol said it has never claimed to be licensed or authorized by MAS, remains permissionless infrastructure with user self-custody, and will continue engaging constructively with regulators worldwide. As Hyperliquid's core team is largely based in Singapore, the MAS listing could prompt the team to consider relocating its operations. Binance and Bybit were previously added to the MAS Investor Alert List as well. — link

text: Why Apple and Microsoft Are Raising Prices: The AI-Driven Memory Chip Crunch > Apple and Microsoft are raising prices on some of their core products as soaring memory and storage chip costs drive up manufacturing expenses. > Memory chips, including DRAM (working memory) and NAND (flash storage), are critical components that power everything from smartphones, PCs, and gaming consoles to AI data centers. > The AI boom has fundamentally changed the memory market. Tech giants are securing massive, multi-year supplies of memory chips for AI infrastructure, paying premium prices to guarantee capacity. > In response, memory manufacturers are prioritizing higher-margin AI orders, leaving less DRAM and NAND available for consumer electronics and automobiles. > The supply squeeze has sent memory prices higher, with DRAM experiencing the steepest increases, while NAND prices have also risen rapidly, pushing up production costs across electronics industry. > As a result, companies including Apple and Microsoft are passing some of those higher costs on to consumers through product price increases. > Apple CEO Tim Cook said the company is doing its best to shield customers from higher prices but acknowledged the situation has become unsustainable, telling The Wall Street Journal: "I've never seen anything like it in any area in over 40 years." Sources:

text: FT: Binance to Stop Serving EU Clients Next Week After Failing to Obtain MiCA Licence According to FT, Binance has notified EU clients that it will stop providing services to them from next week after failing to obtain a MiCA licence. From July 1, crypto companies operating in the EU must hold a MiCA licence or risk penalties. Binance's application for a bloc-wide licence in Greece was rejected last week, and it now plans to apply through France. Customers in Poland, Italy, Spain and France have received withdrawal instructions. Binance said some users may be affected before July 1, but it is not asking users to withdraw funds by then, adding that user assets remain safe and secure. — link

After reviewing known hot wallets on Arkham/TRM, he said the exchange's reserves appear to lack large-cap tokens such as ETH, USDT and SOL, indicating it is likely facing liquidity issues. AscendEX was founded by George (Jing) Cao and Ariel Ling in 2018 and was reportedly hacked by Lazarus Group for $78 million in December 2021. — link

text: MSTR Stress Test Says No "Death Spiral," but BTC Per Share Could Collapse Analyst Adam Livingston said he ran a three-year stress test on MSTR, assuming BTC falls to $26,611 by month six, mNAV drops below 0.50x, capital markets close, and BTC must be sold to service senior debt. The model shows the claim ratio rising from 41.5% to 96.7%, common equity BTC per share falling from 138,161 sats to 7,884 sats, and MSTR's modeled share price dropping to $1.01. After cash is exhausted in month nine, MSTR would need to sell 115,727 BTC over three years. In the final scenario, MSTR still holds 731,636 BTC, with mNAV at 1.40x. Livingston said the model shows the main risk is compression in common equity BTC per share, rather than "instant bankruptcy" or a "death spiral." — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: BitGo to Cut Nearly 15% of Workforce, Shift Focus to Stablecoins and AI Infrastructure BitGo CEO Mike Belshe said the company will cut nearly 15% of its workforce. He said BitGo needs to focus more closely on core areas including security, trading, stablecoins, settlement, and AI-driven infrastructure as the ecosystem and the way financial services are built evolve. Affected employees have been notified directly by management and HR. Belshe said the layoffs are a one-time action, with no further cuts currently expected. — link
